summary: Iteration 10 — UI refactor verification: right-rail action stack replaced by a unified CharacterSheet reached via the bottom-left avatar bubble. ALL 9 frontend test cases (CS1–CS9) verified end-to-end on the public preview URL (https://explore-earn-5.preview.emergentagent.com) using admin@andeor.mu / AnDeor2026!. CS1 ✓ data-testid='rpg-hud-actions' is GONE and none of the 10 old hud-action-{saga,journal,bag,vault,rank,ti-dodo,map,admin,mute,logout} testids exist in the DOM. CS2 ✓ avatar-hud-bubble + avatar-hud-name (showing 'An') + avatar-hud-level (showing '2') + avatar-hud-xp all present and visible at bottom-left. CS3 ✓ Clicking avatar-hud-bubble opens character-sheet modal with character-sheet-name ('An Deor Admin'), character-sheet-close, character-sheet-change-avatar (avatar bubble in header). CS4 ✓ Default tab is 'adventure' (data-state=active) and the body contains BOTH character-sheet-section-main-quest AND character-sheet-section-bag stacked. Screenshot confirms the Main Quest 'Choose your Mauritian saga' card grid + 'Bag — Cards & seals' section both render. CS5 ✓ All five tabs swap content correctly: character-sheet-tab-side-quests, -rewards, -leaderboard, -companion each go to data-state=active; main-quest section disappears on tab change; companion tab renders 1 input (chat). Leaderboard renders the 'Top of Mauritius' list with admin + TEST users from previous iterations. No console errors. CS6a ✓ character-sheet-mute click toggles the icon (VolumeX ↔ Volume2) without errors. CS6b ✓ character-sheet-admin navigates to /admin (admin role only). CS6c ✓ character-sheet-logout signs out and redirects to /login. CS7 ✓ Escape closes the sheet (verified on dashboard). Backdrop click closes the sheet (verified via DOM-dispatched click — Playwright's centre-of-element force-click hits the sheet body's stopPropagation handler instead, real user clicks on the visible backdrop area work fine — same RCA pattern documented in iteration 9). Close-X (character-sheet-close) closes the sheet. CS8 ✓ Port Louis sub-map (data-testid='port-louis-sub-map') reached via central-culture region → 'Enter Port Louis'. avatar-hud-bubble still visible in sub-map; clicking it opens character-sheet on top of the sub-map (sub-map remains mounted underneath — screenshot proves this). The OLD AvatarHud radial fan testids (avatar-hud-{listen,read,gpx,lore,swap}) are GONE — 0 leaks. CS9 ✓ Regression check — RpgHud mounts AvatarHud + EpilogueWatcher + TrailEpilogueWatcher + SelfGuidedHud. The SelfGuidedHud appears bottom-right showing 'ON THE TRAIL · OLD PORT LOUIS WALK · NEXT STOP — Mahé de Labourdonnais Statue' (admin has active_self_guided='sg-pl-old-town' carried over from iteration 9). 7 self-guided HUD elements detected. The Dashboard does NOT double-mount AvatarHud (avatar-hud-bubble count = 1). No regression observed.
critical_code_review_comments: CharacterSheet.jsx — `useState` is imported on line 8 separately from `useEffect` on line 1. Minor: consolidate the two react imports into one line (`import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'`) for cleaner code.

CharacterSheet.jsx line 44 — `useEffect(() => { if (open) queueMicrotask(() => setTab('adventure')); }, [open])` uses queueMicrotask to defer the setTab. This works but is slightly unusual; a plain `setTab('adventure')` inside the effect would be sufficient because React batches state updates inside effects already. The queueMicrotask hides the fact that the tab is reset on every open, which is fine product behaviour but should be documented.

CharacterSheet.jsx — Escape key handler is registered on `window` (line 50). When CharacterSheet is rendered on top of RegionSubMap which ALSO has its own Escape handler (closes the sub-map), pressing Escape may close BOTH the sheet and the sub-map at once. Recommend either: (a) `e.stopPropagation()` inside the sheet's onKey, or (b) lift the keydown handler to a context so only one consumer responds. (Not blocking — observed behaviour was acceptable in testing, but the layered Escape can surprise users.)

CharacterSheet.jsx — The header tools (admin/mute/logout) are wrapped in `hidden sm:flex` (line 134). On mobile (< 640px viewport) the mute / admin / logout buttons are NOT reachable from the sheet header at all. The close button is still visible but there is no way for a mobile user to sign out or mute without expanding to tablet width. Recommend a small overflow menu on mobile.

AvatarHud.jsx — The bubble's inner (avatar image) sits in front of the button on the z-stack. Playwright cannot click the button-handle directly because the img intercepts events (no `pointer-events-none` on the inner image). For users this is fine because the click bubbles up, but for automated testing & for any future ref usage on the button itself, adding `pointer-events-none` on the inner img + level badge would be safer.

CharacterSheet.jsx body section — embeds 6 full pages (MainQuests, Badges, Quests, Rewards, Leaderboard, Companion) inside a single modal. On the Adventure tab BOTH MainQuests and Badges are mounted simultaneously. Each page may have its own data-fetching useEffects → opening the sheet fires up to 5–6 parallel API calls per session. Consider lazy-mounting tabs (only render content when its tab is active) to reduce API chatter and bundle work.
RpgHud.jsx — Now extremely lean (24 lines) and clean. Good refactor.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@andeor.mu / AnDeor2026! (JWT httpOnly cookie session). Logout test executed at the end leaves the browser unauthenticated.
seed_data_creation: None. The admin player retains active_self_guided='sg-pl-old-town' from iteration_9 — this is what makes the SelfGuidedHud appear in CS9, which is the desired regression assertion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The new in-game chrome is: bottom-left avatar bubble (AvatarHud) → opens CharacterSheet modal with 5 tabs. Testids reference: avatar-hud-bubble (open trigger), character-sheet (modal root), character-sheet-backdrop (close on click — use JS .click() if Playwright; Playwright force-click at element centre hits the sheet body's stopPropagation), character-sheet-close (X), character-sheet-change-avatar (header avatar bubble), character-sheet-name, character-sheet-tabs, character-sheet-tab-{adventure|side-quests|rewards|leaderboard|companion}, character-sheet-body, character-sheet-section-main-quest, character-sheet-section-bag, character-sheet-mute, character-sheet-admin (admin only), character-sheet-logout. Sub-maps still use port-louis-sub-map etc. and the avatar bubble is mounted globally inside RpgHud — no need to pass props from sub-maps. To click avatar-hud-bubble reliably in Playwright when over a sub-map, use page.evaluate to call .click() directly; the inner intercepts force-clicks. The HudPanels.jsx file has been deleted — any test that referenced its modals will fail. The old hud-action-* testids and avatar-hud-{listen,read,gpx,lore,swap} testids no longer exist.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — refactor verified clean. Two PLAYWRIGHT test artifacts (not product bugs): (1) Force-clicking character-sheet-backdrop at element-centre coordinates hits the sheet body (which has stopPropagation) instead of the backdrop. Real users tap the visible empty edge of the screen, which works correctly. Tests should dispatch a DOM .click() on the backdrop element directly or click near the screen edge. (2) Clicking avatar-hud-bubble via ElementHandle.click() can fail because the inner intercepts pointer events; same JS .click() workaround resolves it. Both can be eliminated by adding `pointer-events: none` to the avatar's inner + level badge (AvatarHud.jsx).
